title: ctenopteris contigua
intro: Ctenopteris contigua is a species of fern native to New Zealand, characterized by its delicate fronds and ability to thrive in a range of environments. Traditionally, the plant has been used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Ctenopteris contigua are said to include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water, and it is also used in traditional remedies for skin conditions and respiratory issues.
